LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT AUSSY MANUHU'S DEMURRER TO UNLIMITED CIVIL COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES BY LUKASZ FILINSKI. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE ANY CLAIMS IN A CIVIL COMPLAINT BUT WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO ALLEGING SOME OR ALL OF THE SAME CLAIMS IN THE PARTIES' PENDING FAMILY LAW CASE, FILINKSI V. DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-30-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AUSSY MANUHU'S DEMURRER TO UNLIMITED CIVIL COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES BY LUKASZ FILINSKI

Appearances: Nathan L. Scheg of the Ironhorse Law Group (925.974.3304) for defendant and moving party; Lauren Mullee of the Law Office of Lauren Mullee (949.570.0701) for plaintiff and responding party.

Plaintiff did not provide timely notice of the intent to argue against the tentative ruling. After some discussion, the court allows arguments to proceed. At the conclusion of arguments, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Aussy Manuhu's demurrer to all ten causes of action in the civil complaint filed by plaintiff Lukasz Filinski is sustained without leave to amend to allege any claims in a civil complaint but without prejudice to alleging some or all of the same claims in the parties' pending family law case, Filinksi v. Manuhu FDI-22-797233. Because the primary allegation underlying all of Mr. Filinski's alleged civil claims concerns the characterization and entitlement to property that is also at issue In the family law case, the parties' must litigate those claims in the family law case, not a civil action. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
